text.title: langchain-production-patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Hardening rules for production LangChain chains: fallbacks, retries, configurable fields, batch, dependency pinning, observability pairing.

rule.statement: Wire .with_fallbacks([backup]) when ≥2 providers are available AND target_uptime ≥ 0.99.
rule.rationale: Single-provider outages of 30-60min are routine; a non-fallback chain trips your SLA on the first one.

rule.statement: Cap retry.max_attempts at 5 with exponential backoff + jitter. Linear backoff or no cap turns a transient blip into a retry storm.
rule.rationale: Unbounded retries multiply load on the failing service, often making the outage worse and burning your budget.

rule.statement: Expose model + temperature via .configurable_fields so you can A/B and swap models at runtime without redeploy.
rule.rationale: Hot-swap saves you a deploy cycle when one model regresses.

rule.statement: Pin LangChain (and embedding model versions) to exact-hash via uv/pip-tools when dep_policy=locked. Minor releases have changed defaults in LangChain.
rule.rationale: Silent dep upgrades produced subtly different outputs the day after CI ran; only hash-pinning guarantees reproducibility.

rule.statement: Use chain.batch with return_exceptions=True; correlate results by input index, never by output order.
rule.rationale: Partial failures reorder outputs; downstream consumers assuming order silently see swapped results.

rule.statement: Alert when fallback hit-rate exceeds 5% over a 1-hour window. Silent successful fallback masks a dead primary.
rule.rationale: Fallbacks are safety nets, not free uptime; you need to know when they fire.

rule.statement: Pair every with_retry with an outer circuit breaker. Retries × tools = turn explosion otherwise (see [[max-turns-circuit-breaker]]).
rule.rationale: Tool-using agents amplify retry cost: each retry can spawn N more tool calls.

rule.statement: Require LangSmith tracing for every chain with fallbacks. Untraced fallbacks make root-cause analysis impossible.
rule.rationale: Without traces you cannot tell whether the primary or backup served the request, let alone why.
